title: hypericum mutilum var. minus
intro: Hypericum mutilum var. minus, also known as dwarf St. John's Wort, is a low-growing perennial plant that belongs to the Hypericum genus. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Hypericum mutilum var. minus are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, leading to various health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and enhanced immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, making it a versatile remedy for various health concerns.
